title: claytonia gayana
intro: Claytonia gayana, also known as Mexican miner's lettuce, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. It belongs to the Portulacaceae family and is used for its medicinal and culinary properties. Claytonia gayana has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and indigestion, due to its high content of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, salve, or added to salads, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies and a nutritious ingredient in cooking.
